summary.goal: Remove .memex/graph.json entirely; derive root from unique parent-less node; simplify memex-check workflow (closes #50)
decisions: Removed the Graph struct, save_graph/load_graph/graph_path, and the graph.json file from the repo. Root is derived as the unique node whose parent_ids is empty — that single rule replaces both 'graph.root_id is the source of truth for the root' and 'graph.json must be updated on every node create'.

context.rs requires exactly one root (errors on 0 or >1 parent-less nodes via find_root); graph.rs view stays permissive (renders each parent-less node as a separate tree). Different needs: context produces a single linear payload; view is exploratory and should still render whatever's on disk.

memex-check workflow now: keep 'new node JSON added' editorial gate; replace the two graph.json checks with 'every UUID in parent_ids references an existing .memex/nodes/<uuid>.json at HEAD' (or, for empty parent_ids, exactly one parent-less node post-merge).

rejected_approaches.description: Restore edges to the Graph struct so memex node create writes graph.json again (Option A in PR #48's body)
rejected_approaches.reason: Would undo b95077e3's deliberate single-source-of-truth decision. The bug is that b95077e3 stopped halfway, not that it went too far. Completing it is the correct fix.

rejected_approaches.description: Keep graph.json with just root_id (drop only the dead version field)
rejected_approaches.reason: root_id is derivable from the unique parent-less node. Keeping a file that exists only to record a derivable fact preserves the temptation to hand-edit it (which is exactly what #50 was about).

rejected_approaches.description: Add an is_root: bool field to ConversationNode
rejected_approaches.reason: Creates a second source of truth for what "root" means (the field vs. parent_ids being empty). Same dual-representation problem b95077e3 was trying to remove.

open_threads: Existing user checkouts may still have a stale .memex/graph.json on disk — load_graph is gone so it is just ignored, but the file lingers until the user does git pull. No migration logic added. Document in README if/when this matters; cheap to add later.

graph view silently renders multiple trees if the graph has multiple parent-less nodes. Post-init that only happens via hand-editing — a warning printed in that case would surface the corruption faster than waiting for a context call to error.
